desc:
The road goes on, taking you to strange places. This area, surrounded by green
fields, seems quite peaceful though. The road continues to the west and east.
Further in the west, you can see a small village of tiny houses.
name: The East Road

desc:
You are travelling on the Old East Road, with green fields all around you. To
the north you can see the Bridgefields, and far in the west you see a small
hobbit village. The road goes west-east here.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road is quiet and peaceful around here. A small fence is south of the road,
preventing you from entering a huge garden. To the north you see a small hill,
and the road continues west and east.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road goes on, leading to unknown and mysterious places. The road is now
crossing a region of peaceful fields, where the air is clear and quite
enjoyable. All around you, green fields extend into the distance. A small river
is cutting the fields north of here, and a small farm is to the south.
name: The East Road

desc:
You now stand on the Old East Road...it looks well rutted from the many who have
passed this way. The road continues on in an east-west direction. There is
vegetation to the north of the road and traverse is possible there.
name: The East Road

desc:
You are standing on the Old East Road in the extreme east of the Shire.
Northwest of here you see the Bridgefields, and south of here you can see a wide
and heavily used path. The road leads on east and west, Brandywine bridge is
just east of here.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road winds its way through a light forest. One might wonder where the this
road leads, but for now there seems an odd sense of safety here at least
compared to the forest.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You are travelling along on the Old East Road, which winds its way through a
light forest here. There seems to be some fields beyond the light forest to the
north, but there is a shadow over the forest in the south.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You are adventuring on a well travelled road going east-west. The road at this
point is enclosed by woodland to the north and south. The forest, though light
and young along the roadside, appears to age and thicken further to the south.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You are adventuring on a well travelled road going east-west. The road at this
point is enclosed by woodland to the north and south. The forest, though light
and young along the roadside, appears to age and thicken further to the south.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You are adventuring on the well travelled Old East Road. The road at this point
turns southward. There is a light forest bordering the road to the north and
west of here. Further to the southwest you see an old and wise forest.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You are adventuring on a well travelled road going east-west. The road at this
point is enclosed by woodland to the north and south. The forest, though light
and young along the roadside, appears to age and thicken further to the
southwest.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here the road runs east-west and is bordered by a high bank that leads to
plains. The road is rutted and there are many pot-holes filled with water from
the recent rains. Going east the road sinks down into a ravine.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here the road runs east-west and is bordered by a high bank that leads to
plains. The road is rutted and there are many pot-holes filled with water from
the recent rains. The road turns slightly but still heads mostly in the
east-west direction.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here the road runs east-west and is bordered by a high bank that leads to
plains. The road is rutted and there are many pot-holes filled with water from
the recent rains. The road turns slightly but still heads mostly in the
east-west direction.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here the road turns northward. You see that wagon wheels have dug deep ruts into
the road and that they are filled with water from recent heavy rains. You feel
that you are not too far from town.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here the road runs east-west. You see that wagon wheels have dug deep ruts into
the road that are filled with water from recent heavy rains. There are many
potholes, and the road is bordered by high plains to the south, and a light
forest to the north.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You tread your way carefully on sloping ground which rises steadily from west to
east. Here the hills of Scary end in a sharp incline on your north. On the
southern side, you can see a tangle of tree-tops a little way below. You wonder
what may be hidden under, but there is no way down from this point.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have reached a high plateau which extends for some distance to the east and
south. Gazing north-east from here, you can see the water of the River
Brandywine at a short distance off, and a village just by it. The hills
themselves end in a sharp incline on your north.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a high plateau which extends for some distance to the east, west and
south. Gazing north-east from here, you can see the water of the River
Brandywine at a short distance off, and a village just by it. The hills
themselves end in a sharp incline on your north.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a high plateau which extends for some distance to the east, west and
south. Gazing north-east from here, you can see the water of the River
Brandywine at a short distance off, and a village just by it. The hills
themselves end in a sharp incline on your north.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have come to the north-eastern end of the high plateau - indeed of the
entire range of hills. The village of Dwaling is now visible at your feet, and
the River Brandywine looks like a little curvy rift in the surrounding green
terrain. You look for a way down, but the slopes on your north and east are too
steep.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
This is part of the River Brandywine which forms the east border of the Shire.
The water is cool and clear, and seems to be home to a great variety of fishes.
North of here, the River appears to constrict suddenly, causing the water flow
upstream to be much more rapid than from this point onwards. Going further
upstream is impossible, for it is all boaters on this water can do to keep small
crafts from being carried back south by the current.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are on the River Brandywine which forms the east border of the Shire. The
River forks here, as some of the north-south main flow is diverted into a
smaller west branch. There seems to be a proper landing and a bustling village
further to the west.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. The River makes an abrupt turn in its course from north to east at
this juncture. West of here, you see a sandy, pebbled beach which looks like a
safe place to land if you so wish. The sand gives way to a flat stretch of tall
grass a little further inland.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. At this juncture, the River makes an abrupt turn in its course from
west to south. The water looks cool and clear, and seems to be home to a great
variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. The River makes an abrupt turn in its course from north to east at
this juncture. West of here, you see a sandy, pebbled beach which looks like a
safe place to land if you so wish. The sand gives way to a flat stretch of tall
grass a little further inland.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. At this juncture, the River makes an abrupt turn in its course from
west to south. The water looks cool and clear, and seems to be home to a great
variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. Here the River flows in a straight course from north to south. You
see a flat stretch of tall grass rolling away from the west bank, but there is
no suitable spot to land your craft. The water looks cool and clear, and seems
to be home to a great variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. Here the River flows in a straight course from north to south. You
see a flat stretch of tall grass rolling away from the west bank, but there is
no suitable spot to land your craft. The water looks cool and clear, and seems
to be home to a great variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. Here the River flows in a straight course from north to south. You
see a flat stretch of tall grass rolling away from the west bank, but there is
no suitable spot to land your craft. The water looks cool and clear, and seems
to be home to a great variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. The River makes an abrupt turn to the north and east at this
juncture, but flows straight for some miles beyond in both directions. The water
looks cool and clear, and seems to be home to a great variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. Here the River flows in a winding course from east to south. You see
a flat stretch of tall grass rolling away from the north bank, but there is no
suitable spot to land your craft. The water looks cool and clear, and seems to
be home to a great variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
This is the River Brandywine, which forms the east border of the Shire. A steep
headland rises to your northwest, on which you can seem some great old trees,
proud and sad; a stream trickles down from above, splashing into glistening
droplets as it hits the rocks. The river's flow washes around its base, turning
westward.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. On your north-west are the hills which tower like formidable foes,
forcing the River to turn and twist its way around their base. The flow of the
water is more rapid here, and the stronger current rocks your small craft
playfully.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. On your west is the start - or end - of some hills which tower like
formidable foes, forcing the River to turn and twist its way around their base.
The flow of the water is more rapid here, and the stronger current rocks your
small craft playfully.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are on a high plateau which extends for some distance to the north and west.
On your south and east, the ground slopes down to lower terrain which looks the
same. You ponder the wisdom of continuing.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have descended on a low ridge at the eastern end of the hills of Scary. At
your feet, a grassy stretch of field borders these hills both to the north and
east. The River Brandywine further to the east looks like a little curvy rift in
the surrounding green terrain. You know the village of Dwaling lies a little to
your north-west, but your view in that direction is blocked by the rising hills.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a high plateau which extends for some distance to the north, east and
west. On your south is a sheer drop down to a tangle of tree-tops. You wonder
what may be hidden under, but there is no way down from here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a high plateau which extends for some distance to the north, east and
west. On your south is a sheer drop down to a tangle of tree-tops. You wonder
what may be hidden under, but there is no way down from here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a high plateau which extends for some distance to the north and east.
On your west and south are sheer drops down to a tangle of tree-tops. You wonder
what may be hidden under, but there is no way down from here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are in a beautiful valley which teems with life - the life of trees and
flowers and grass and small animals. Yet you feel a strange loneliness and
sadness here, as if the valley itself is mourning for something lost to it. The
valley continues to your west, where the trees seem to thin out a little. Your
attention, however, is drawn to the particularly thick growth of trees on your
south which completely blocks passage in that direction.
name: A Valley

desc:
You are on a low hilltop deep in the folds of the hills of Scary, where no
external sights or sounds intrude. Everywhere you look, you see grass, shrubs
and more hills. On your west is a sheer drop to a tangle of tree-tops a little
way below. You wonder what may be hidden under, but there is no way down from
here. The slopes in all other directions are climbable, but they seem to lead to
identical terrain.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a high hilltop deep in the folds of the hills of Scary, where no
external sights or sounds intrude. Everywhere you look, you see grass, shrubs
and more hills. On your south is a sheer drop to what looks like a beautiful
valley. The slopes in all other directions are climbable, but they seem to lead
to identical terrain.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have come to a narrow ridge at the eastern end of the hills of Scary. At
your feet, a grassy stretch of field borders these hills both to the north and
east, while the River Brandywine further to the east looks like a little curvy
rift in the surrounding green terrain. The ridge extends to your south.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have come to the southern edge of the narrow ridge. This may be the most
scenic spot in the entire range of hills, for a different view greets you in
each direction. On your west is a sheer drop down to what looks like a beautiful
valley, while on your east a grassy stretch meets the foot of the hills. South
you can gaze directly down on the water of the River Brandywine.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a low hilltop deep in the folds of the hills of Scary, where no
external sights or sounds intrude. Everywhere you look, you see grass, shrubs
and more hills. On your east is a sheer drop to what looks like a beautiful
valley. The slopes in all other directions are climbable, but they seem to lead
to identical terrain.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a higher hilltop which extends to the west. Wild grass and shrubs
cover the ground as elsewhere. On your north is a sheer drop down to a tangle of
tree-tops some way below. You wonder what may be hidden under. East of here the
ground slopes down.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a higher hilltop which extends to the east and west. Wild grass and
shrubs cover the ground as elsewhere. On your north is a sheer drop down to a
tangle of tree-tops some way below. You wonder what may be hidden under.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a higher hilltop which extends to the east and west. Wild grass and
shrubs cover the ground as elsewhere. On your north is a sheer drop down to a
tangle of tree-tops some way below. You wonder what may be hidden under.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have climbed up on a higher hilltop which extends to the east. Wild grass
and shrubs cover the ground as elsewhere. On your north is a sheer drop down to
a tangle of tree-tops some way below. You wonder what may be hidden under.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have come to the southern end of the hills of Scary. Here the terrain seems
rockier, and there is a noticeable thinning of the grass which covers the
ground. Looking down the steep southern slope, you see what appears to be a
quarry, but there is no way down.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have entered an old quarry which appears to have seen the last of its
excavation days long ago. Rusted, broken tools lie in a small pile in a corner.
In another corner, some flat rocks have been arranged to serve as table and
stools. This must be where the hobbits rest from work.
name: Old Quarry

desc:
Here begins the flat stretch of fields that the Shire hobbits name the
Bridgefields. From this end the thick grass stretches away to the east and
south. A wide path lies west of here, and a rocky ridge rises up north.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
This is a large and flat field on the east side of the road leading to the
hills. The grass reaches your ankles and some flowers grow around. The field
continues north, south or east.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are enjoying yourself on The Water, which flows slowly to the east. The
river cuts through the southern part of the Bridgefields, and you can see the
road some distance to the south. There seems to be a rich animal life in this
area, with fish in the river and animals in the fields. You can hear the sound
of some hobbit children playing as well.
name: The Water

desc:
You are enjoying yourself on The Water, which flows slowly to the east. The
river cuts through the southern part of the Bridgefields, and you can see the
road some distance to the south. There seems to be a rich animal life in this
area, with fish in the river and animals in the fields. You can hear the sound
of some hobbit children playing as well.
name: The Water

desc:
You are enjoying yourself on The Water, which flows slowly to the east. The
river cuts through the southern part of the Bridgefields, and you can see the
road some distance to the south. There seems to be a rich animal life in this
area, with fish in the river and animals in the fields. You can hear the sound
of some hobbit children playing as well. The Water joins the much larger river
Brandywine just east of here, and they continue southwards towards the
southeastern parts of the Shire.
name: The Water

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is too wide to cross
easily even here. This is the border to the Shire, to the west you see the
homeland of the hobbits, known as Halflings to some. It has a friendly look, and
no evil seems to have infested the land. Small ripples in the water suggest that
there are fish in the river. The Water joins the Brandywine here, and you can
continue west on The Water or east on the Brandywine.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is too wide to cross
easily even here. This is the border to the Shire, to the west you see the
homeland of the hobbits, known as Halflings to some. It has a friendly look, and
no evil seems to have infested the land. Small ripples in the water suggest that
there are fish in the river.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is too wide to cross
easily even here. This is the border to the Shire, to the west you see the
homeland of the Hobbits, known as Halflings to some. It has a friendly look, and
no evil seems to have infested the land. Small ripples in the water suggest that
there are fish in the river.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is too wide to cross
easily even here. This is the border to the Shire, to the west you see the
homeland of the hobbits, known as Halflings to some. It has a friendly look, and
no evil seems to have infested the land. Small ripples in the water suggest that
there are fish in the river.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is too wide to cross
easily even here. This is the border to the Shire, to the west you see the
homeland of the hobbits, known as Halflings to some. It has a friendly look, and
no evil seems to have infested the land. Small ripples in the water suggest that
there are fish in the river. The bank on both sides seems to be easily
climbable.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is too wide to cross
easily even here. This is the border to the Shire, to the west you see the
homeland of the hobbits, known as Halflings to some. It has a friendly look, and
no evil seems to have infested the land. Small ripples in the water suggest that
there are fish in the river.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You continue slowly along the River Brandywine which forms the east border of
the Shire. From here, you can see the Bridgefields rolling away from the west
bank of the River, but embarkation is out of the question. The water is cool and
clear, and seems to be home to a great variety of fishes.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
The road makes a bend here, going west-north. A light forest is bordering the
road on both sides, but the hedgerow blocks your way to the south. You can
barely glimpse a darker shadow to the southeast, and it causes shivers of fear
down your back.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road makes a bend here. A light forest is bordering the road on both sides,
but you can sense a shadow behind the forest to the southeast.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road is winding its way through a light forest here. You wonder where the
road might lead you, but you feel this is a safe area. The shadow in the
southeast is disconcerting, however.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You are standing in a light forest bordering the road. Further north you can see
some fields, and the road is just south of here. South of the road the forest
seems to darken, as if a shadow is lying over it.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
You are at the western end of a light forest which surrounds the Old East road
on its way east. Directly west of here is the River Brandywine, and there is
even a small pier here for easy access with a small boat. There is a note on a
small wooden post here.
name: Opening in the Forest

desc:
Walking through the green fields you see the River Brandywine to the west. Small
flowers spot the land, and to the southeast a forest is visible. To the west is
the eastern part of the Shire, home of the halflings.
name: Green Fields

desc:
Walking through the green fields you see the River Brandywine to the west. Small
flowers spot the land, and to the southeast a forest is visible. To the west is
the eastern part of the Shire, home of the halflings.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are in the outskirts of the light forest. North and west are endless fields.
In the west you behold the Brandywine lazily snaking through the field. Beyond
that the eastern part of the Shire. There is a forest to the east and southwest
of here. The road is visible to the southeast.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
You are walking in the Bridgefields, which extend north from the road all the
way to the hills in the north. The grass here is green and healthy and is a
perfect environment for animals. Some tracks in the grass suggest that hobbit
children like to play here as well. The Brandywine runs southwards just east of
here, and the small river The Water joins it from the west. Beyond the Water you
see more green fields.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
You are walking in the Bridgefields, which extend north from the road all the
way to the hills in the north. The grass here is green and healthy and is a
perfect environment for animals. Some tracks in the grass suggest that hobbit
children like to play here as well. The Water is slowly flowing eastwards just
south of here, and there is a small spring here which leads into the river.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
Walking through the green fields you see the River Brandywine to the west. Small
flowers spot the land, and to the southeast a forest is visible. To the west is
the eastern part of the Shire, home of the halflings.
name: Green Fields

desc:
Walking through the green fields you see the River Brandywine to the west. Small
flowers spot the land, and to the southeast a forest is visible. To the west is
the eastern part of the Shire, home of the halflings.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are walking in the Bridgefields, which extend north from the road all the
way to the hills in the north. The grass here is green and healthy and is a
perfect environment for animals. Some tracks in the grass suggest that hobbit
children like to play here as well. The Brandywine flows slowly southwards just
east of here, but there is no way to approach it from this point. The fields
continue to the west.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
You are walking in the Bridgefields, which extend north from the road all the
way to the hills in the north. The grass here is green and healthy and is a
perfect environment for animals. Some tracks in the grass suggest that hobbit
children like to play here as well. The Brandywine is east of here, and this
looks like a good place to enter it. The river is much too large to cross
without a boat, though.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
Walking through the green fields you see the River Brandywine to the west. Small
flowers spot the land, and to the southeast a forest is visible. To the west is
the eastern part of the Shire, home of the halflings.
name: Green Fields

desc:
Walking through the green fields you see the River Brandywine to the west. Small
flowers spot the land, and to the southeast a forest is visible. To the west is
the eastern part of the Shire, home of the halflings.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are standing in a light forest bordering the road. Further north you can see
some fields, and the road is just south of here. South of the road the forest
seems to darken, as if a shadow is lying over it.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
You are standing in a light forest bordering the road. Further north you can see
some fields, and the road is just south of here. South of the road the forest
seems to darken, as if a shadow is lying over it.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
Your random paths have led you to the centre of a huge expanse of wild wheat.
You are standing on a slight hill, allowing you to see all the crazy paths
you've made. Silently, you hope that there's no farmer nearby that will
terrorise you for destroying this piece of natural beauty. You decide to head
back to the shelter of the trees.
name: In the Wheat Field

desc:
You've entered a light, young forest that offers safe shelter for the night.
There is a small clearing that looks like the perfect spot to rest and build a
small campfire. An old tree bordering the clearing looks like a good spot to sit
back, relax and stargaze for a bit. It even looks comfortable enough to take a
well deserved nap.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
You come out of the light woodland on the fringes of a field of wild wheat. You
reminisce about your childhood days and begin to make your own pathways through
the natural wheat crop.
name: Wheat Field

desc:
This lonely little house is as badly neglected as the rest of the farm. There is
only enough furniture to make it livable. Everything is covered in dust and it
tingles your nose till you have a sneezing fit. In the corner there lies only a
shabby mattress with one blanket full of holes. The table standing in the centre
of the room is laden with past eaten meals, some look like they have began a
life of their own. You decide to leave this place as quickly as possible.
name: A Small Lonely House

desc:
You are wandering along a path that meanders through the light woodland. The
vegetation on the forest floor makes walking easy on your feet. Around you,
various friendly forest animals go about their business.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
You are adventuring on the well travelled Old East Road. The road at this point
bends to the north. Bordering the road to the east and south is a forest that
seems to age and darken to the southwest.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The path you've been following through the light wood ends here. It joins with a
path going north-south. As you look south, you see the Old East Road is not far
off. Through the light forest to the north you can barely make out what might be
a clearing.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
This path seems to be well worn into the ground. Nothing grows on this path, and
all you can see are sets of footsteps going east and west. Some strange prints
seem to come from the south at this point. The trail heads east and west here
and is bordered by a light forest. The path you've traversed from the south was
so rough to follow that you decide there must be a better way out of here.
name: A Well Trampled Path

desc:
This path seems to lead to a small lonely house, surrounded by a light forest.
The house seems to hardly exist here; the shutters are barely hanging by their
hinges, the door does not close completely, and there appear to be several holes
in the roof where the shingles have fallen away. There are some strange old
tracks that seem to disappear suddenly, while other fresher tracks lead toward
the house.
name: In Front of a Small Lonely House

desc:
This path seems to be well worn into the ground. Nothing grows on this path, and
all you can see are various sets of tracks that lead to the water well that
stands here. Close by, you hear the sounds of pigs that seem to be happily
playing in their slimy mess. To the southeast you see the remains of a barn.
Toward the north, the trail is bordered by a light forest.
name: A Water Well

desc:
Your teeth begin to click together as you've just immersed yourself in chest
deep water that seems to be little above freezing. The water is a bit murky, but
still drinkable. On the bottom of the well you see the broken rope that used to
hold the pail. The walls of the well are made of white stone. You are standing
in well water that flows from an underground spring.
name: Down in the Well

desc:
You are in a thin tract of grass sandwiched between the hills on your west and
the River Brandywine on your east. From the look of the tall, thick grass which
would almost submerge a hobbit, it has been forgotten by the local Shire folk.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in a thin tract of grass sandwiched between the hills on your west and
the River Brandywine on your east. From the look of the tall, thick grass which
would almost submerge a hobbit, it has been forgotten by the local Shire folk.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in a thin tract of grass sandwiched between the hills on your west and
the River Brandywine on your east. From the look of the tall, thick grass which
would almost submerge a hobbit, it has been forgotten by the local Shire folk.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in a thin tract of grass sandwiched between the hills on your west and
the River Brandywine on your east. From the look of the tall, thick grass which

name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in a thin tract of grass sandwiched between the hills on your west and
the River Brandywine on your east. From the look of the tall, thick grass which
would almost submerge a hobbit, it has been forgotten by the local Shire folk.
The grass gradually thins out till it gives way to a sandy beach on your east
from where you can enter the River.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in a thin tract of grass sandwiched between the hills on your west and
the River Brandywine on your east. From the look of the tall, thick grass which

name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in a thin tract of grass sandwiched between the hills on your west and

The grass gradually thins out till it gives way to a sandy beach on your east

name: Grassy Field

desc:
You walk on a narrow trail which seems to have lain undiscovered for ages. Rows
of tall trees line both sides of the trail, leaving just enough room for two to
walk abreast. Overhead the tree branches form a canopy which sways in the wind,
causing the sunlight filtering through to constantly shift and move shadows
around. The trail leads east and west.
name: A Hidden Trail

desc:
You are at the end of a narrow trail which seems to have lain undiscovered for
ages. Rows of tall trees line both sides of the trail, leaving just enough room
for two to walk abreast. Overhead the tree branches form a canopy which sways in
the wind, causing the sunlight filtering through to constantly shift and move
shadows around. The trail leads east.
name: A Hidden Trail

desc:
You walk on a narrow trail which seems to have lain undiscovered for ages. Rows
of tall trees line both sides of the trail, leaving just enough room for two to
walk abreast. Overhead the tree branches form a canopy which sways in the wind,
causing the sunlight filtering through to constantly shift and move shadows
around. The trail leads east and west.
name: A Hidden Trail

desc:
You are in a tunnel which seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably
been worked by hand to what it is now. The walls are wide-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, such that you feel you are walking in a grand passageway.
Carvings and writings in a foreign script decorate the walls at intervals. The
main tunnel makes a turn to the south, while a side tunnel branches off to your
north.
name: A Tunnel in the Hills

desc:
You are in a tunnel which seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably
been worked by hand to what it is now. The walls are wide-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, such that you feel you are walking in a grand passageway.
Carvings and writings in a foreign script decorate the walls at intervals. The
air here is a little stale.
name: A Side Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably been worked by
hand to reach its present shape. The walls are widely-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, giving the impression of being in a grand passageway. Carvings
and writings in an archaic script decorate the walls at invervals. The air here
is a little stale.
name: A Side Tunnel

desc:
You are in a tunnel which seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably
been worked by hand to what it is now. The walls are wide-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, such that you feel you are walking in a grand passageway.
Carvings and writings in a foreign script decorate the walls at intervals. The
air here is a little stale.
name: A Side Tunnel

desc:
You are in a tunnel which seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably
been worked by hand to what it is now. The walls are wide-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, such that you feel you are walking in a grand passageway.
Carvings and writings in a foreign script decorate the walls at intervals. The
air here is a little stale.
name: A Side Tunnel

desc:
You have entered a large natural cavern which seems to have been a store at some
time in the lost past. Plates and cups of fine workmanship are stacked in a
corner, and a few wine barrels sit forgotten in another. Some crumbled remains
of unidentifiable items - which you guess to be food - are scattered over a part
of the floor. Strangely, there is no bad odour.
name: A Cavern

desc:
You are in a tunnel which seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably
been worked by hand to what it is now. The walls are wide-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, such that you feel you are walking in a grand passageway.
Carvings and writings in a foreign script decorate the walls at intervals. The
air here is a little stale. To your east, the ground rushes downwards in a
sudden slope.
name: A Side Tunnel

desc:
You are in a tunnel which seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably
been worked by hand to what it is now. The walls are wide-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, such that you feel you are walking in a grand passageway.
Carvings and writings in a foreign script decorate the walls at intervals. The
air here is a little stale. The ground slopes upwards to your west.
name: A Side Tunnel

desc:
The beautiful valley teems with life. There is a distinct feel of goodness in
the air, and an inexplicable sense of well-being in all the living. Yet the
hushed whispers of the huge, ancient trees sound strangely sad. You spy the
lower ends of rope ladders hanging from the higher branches of some trees. One
particularly huge tree catches your attention.
name: A Beautiful Valley

desc:
You have climbed onto the lower branches of a huge tree. Among the leaves, you
see the end of a rope ladder which goes up into the thick foliage of this
ancient tree. You can just reach the ladder.
name: On a Huge Tree

desc:
You are on some sort of platform nestled cozily among the tree branches. On one
side of it is a light, movable screen, the purpose of which must be to block the
wind. The layer of withered leaves on it tells you no elf has been here for ages
- for there is no question as to who built the flet. You begin to understand the
sorrow of the trees.
name: On a Huge Tree

desc:
This part of the valley juts out from the surrounding hills such that it affords
a clear view both of the sky above and the landscape below. It even appears to
overhang a section of the River Brandywine, for you see water whichever edge you
look down. There is an unmistakably Elven feel to the beauty of the place, and
you feel certain elves must have sat here singing to the stars in times past.
name: Edge of the Valley

desc:
You are standing on a large and flat field. The grass is not too tall and some
flowers can be found. To the north and south the field continues. To the west is
a small north-south road. East you see the Bridgefields.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are walking in the Bridgefields, which extend north from the road all the
way to the hills in the north. The grass here is green and healthy and is a
perfect environment for animals. Some tracks in the grass suggest that hobbit
children like to play here as well. A small river is cutting the fields just
south of here.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
You are walking in the Bridgefields, which extend north from the road all the
way to the hills in the north. The grass here is green and healthy and is a
perfect environment for animals. Some tracks in the grass suggest that hobbit
children like to play here as well. A small river is cutting the fields just
south of here.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
You are enjoying yourself on The Water, which flows slowly to the east. The
river cuts through the southern part of the Bridgefields, and you can see the
road some distance to the south. There seems to be rich animal life in this
area, with fish in the river and animals in the fields. You can hear the sound
of some hobbit children playing as well.
name: The Water

desc:
This village, built on one side of the Road, is formed by little houses of
hobbits. To the north is The Water, and beyond are the hills in the distance.
All around you, you see people walking doing their daily labours. The hobbits
have always been a peaceful race, and this village proves it.
name: The Village of Witfurrows

desc:
You are standing in the edge of the Bridgefields, next to Old East Road. North
of here you can see the small river The Water, running calmly east and west. The
Bridgefields continue to the north and east, though there is a small hill to the
east. West of here you see the village of Witfurrows.
name: On the Side of the Road

desc:
The East Road can be seen trailing across the land just to the south of this
small hill, and to the north lies the Water. The hill itself seems to have been
made the home of a hobbit family, for a small circular door has been built here,
which it seems must lead into a hobbit hole.
name: A Small Hill

desc:
You are standing on the side of the road, between it and the small river The
Water. Beyond the river you see the large Bridgefields. The flow of the river is
slow and calm, and you can take it with a boat. There is a small hill to the
west.
name: On the Side of the Road

desc:
You are standing in the southeastern end of the Bridgefields. The road is south
of here, and The Water runs calmly eastwards towards River Brandywine just north
of here. It would probably be a delightful place to have a boat, but still you
can't see any hobbits on the river.
name: On the Side of the Road

desc:
The River Brandywine runs slowly southwards just east of here. It is large
enough to make crossing without a boat very dangerous. This seems to be a good
spot to enter the river, north of here the riverbank becomes more unfriendly.
The small river The Water meets the Brandywine northwest of here, and further
west and north you see the Bridgefields.
name: By the River Brandywine

desc:
You are standing in the middle of a big field of green and thick grass. You can
see different kinds of flowers too, and some trees. To the north can be seen the
East Road, while south the fields continue.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are standing on a large and flat field. The grass is not too tall and some
flowers can be found. To the north and south the field continues. To the west is
a small road going south-north. East you can see the Bridgefields.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in the Bridgefields, which extend from the hills on your north to the
Brandywine Bridge. The grass is thick, and pretty wild flowers break the
monotony of green around you.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
You are in the Bridgefields, which extend from the hills in the north to the
Brandywine Bridge. The grass is thick, and pretty wild flowers break the
monotony of green around you.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
You are in the Bridgefields, which extend from the hills in the north to the
Brandywine Bridge. The grass is thick, and pretty wild flowers break the
monotony of green around you.
name: Bridgefields

name: Bridgefields

name: Bridgefields

You are walking in the Bridgefields, which extend north from the road all the

name: Bridgefields

desc:
You have come to a low plateau which extends for some distance to your east and
south. Here the terrain seems rockier, and there is a noticeable thinning of the
grass which covers the ground. The slopes in all directions are climbable.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You stand on a low plateau at the edge of the range of hills. Here the terrain
seems rockier, and there is a noticeable thinning of the grass which covers the
ground. On your east and south are sheer drops down to a flat stretch of grass.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
From the foot of the surrounding hills, the Bridgefields extend southwards to
the Brandywine Bridge which crosses the Water. The grass is thick, and pretty
wild flowers break the monotony of green around you.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
You have climbed up on a narrow ridge flanked by sheer drops both east and west.
Peering down your east, you see what looks like a beautiful valley, but there is
no obvious way to reach it. On your west, a grassy field meets the foot of the
hills. The ridge continues to the south.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
From the foot of the surrounding hills, the Bridgefields extend southwards to
the Brandywine Bridge which crosses the Water. The grass is thick, and pretty
wild flowers break the monotony of green around you.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
From the foot of the surrounding hills, the Bridgefields extend southwards to
the Brandywine Bridge which crosses the Water. The grass is thick, and pretty
wild flowers break the monotony of green around you.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
Your entrance is greeted by an overpowering musty smell amidst the flying specks
of dust stirred up by your footsteps. The hole looks dry, and may have once been
comfortable, but it stands empty and cobweb-covered now. By the shape of it, you
know it to have been built by hobbits, and you wonder what caused the
inhabitants to abandon their home.
name: Abandoned Hobbit Hole

desc:
Your entrance is greeted by an overpowering musty smell amidst the flying specks
of dust stirred up by your footsteps. The hole looks dry, and may have once been
comfortable, but it stands empty and cobweb-covered now. By the shape of it, you
know it to have been built by hobbits, and you wonder what caused the
inhabitants to abandon their home.
name: Abandoned Hobbit Hole

name: Abandoned Hobbit Hole

name: Abandoned Hobbit Hole

desc:
You are in a tunnel which seems to have been formed naturally, but has probably
been worked by hand to what it is now. The walls are wide-spaced apart and the
ceiling is high, such that you feel you are walking in a grand passageway.
Carvings and writings in a foreign script decorate the walls at intervals. The
main tunnel makes a turn to the north, while a side tunnel branches off to your
west.
name: A Tunnel in the Hills

desc:
To your immense surprise and delight, you emerge in a beautiful valley which
teems with life. There is a distinct feel of goodness in the air, and an
inexplicable sense of well-being in all the living. Yet the hushed whispers of
the huge, ancient trees sound strangely sad. You spy the lower ends of rope
ladders hanging from the higher branches of some trees.
name: A Beautiful Valley

desc:
You're standing in the eastern end of the huge green fields. The road is just
north of here, going west-east. A wheat field is to the south, and there is a
farm to the east. There are a few flowers among the grass as well, making the
place really beautiful.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are standing in the huge garden next to the farm. Whoever maintains this
garden sure knows how to do it, there are beautiful flowers and plants neatly
arranged all over the place. The sweet smell of flowers fills your lungs, and
you feel like staying here forever. A cute little fence blocks your way north
and west. The garden continues to the south.
name: In a Huge Garden

desc:
You are standing in a small farm, which seems to be quite a common building in
the Shire. To the west is a huge, beautiful garden, and a small backyard is to
the east. You can hear the sound of hobbit children playing in the garden, and
you have to stop for a moment just to feel the refreshing atmosphere.
name: In a Farm

desc:
This is the backyard of the small farm you just were in. This seems to be a nice
place to take a nap and enjoy the nature. In the east you can see the path to
Stock, and beyond it the River Brandywine slowly flows southwards. A cute, small
fence is surrounding the entire farm, and the only way you can go is back west.
name: The Backyard

desc:
A new road opens up, combining the East Road and the Path to Stock. It's still
far to the village of Stock, but the Brandywine Bridge, leading to others lands,
is near. The old forest looms to the east, but for the moment you can't seem to
find a way towards it.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is slowly growing bigger
on its way to the southwest. The river is now well inside the borders of the
Shire, and it is indeed a peaceful place. Green fields and small forests border
the river on both sides, and small rings on the water show that there are fish
in the river as well.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are on a dusty road, behind the north-gate. The High Hay runs down to the
Brandywine here, a tall and thick defensive hedge. On the other side of the
river the vast green fields of the Shire strech out, while to the east the Old
Forest looms just beyond the hedge. This road meets the Old East Road north of
here, by the bridge.
name: Behind the North-Gate of Buckland

desc:
You have entered the luxuriously furnished office of the mayor of Newbury. A
finely crafted oak desk occupies the centre of the room. Several lonely
unfinished documents lie upon the desk as evidence of the mayor's frequent
absence. The walls are paneled with a very fine light brown wood that almost
seems to glow. A masterfully crafted suit of plate armour rests against the wall
of the office. There is a colourful oil painting of the mayor hung upon the
western wall.
name: Mayor of Newbury's Office

desc:
You are standing at the edge of a ancient forest, it looks older than anything
you've seen before. The trees look twisted strangely alive. To the south the
forest looks even darker, and there is an unmistakable scent of evil in the air.
You have the feeling that something is lurking for you in the depths of the
forest. You'd better get out of here quickly!
name: In the Outskirts of the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing in a light part of the forest bordering the road. To the
southeast the forest seems to be much darker, and it almost seems to be alive.
Some evil source must be in the centre of it, and you don't really want to go in
there. The road is north and west of here.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
You are standing at the edge of a ancient forest, it looks older than anything
you've seen before. The trees look twisted strangely alive. To the south the
forest looks even darker, and there is an unmistakable scent of evil in the air.
You have the feeling that something is lurking for you in the depths of the
forest. You'd better get out of here quickly!
name: In the Outskirts of the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing in a light part of the forest bordering the road. To the
southeast the forest seems to be much darker, and it almost seems to be alive.
Some evil source must be in the centre of it, and you don't really want to go in
there. The road is north and west of here.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, and are now directly under the
Brandywine Bridge. To the south you see the southeastern part of the Shire,
which looks as green and fertile as ever.
name: Under the Brandywine Bridge

desc:
The River Brandywine and the Brandywine Bridge define the eastern border of the
Shire, home of the Hobbits. The Shire stretches out west of here; it is a very
rich and fertile land. The Brandywine Bridge is a solid and well-maintained
arched stone bridge built by the kings of Arnor many centuries ago. You notice a
very important sign here.
name: Brandywine Bridge

desc:
These plains are wide open and are fairly level. The grass here is about calf
length and blow gently with the breeze. Walking here is fairly easy. In the sky
you can see various birds flying high over head, you can also hear them calling
to each other. Far to the north you see a dark line that could be the trees
bordering the road. You are wandering around staring up at the sky at the birds
and you don't notice that you are not walking in a set direction. You just seem
to be taking a relaxing walk through the plains.
name: Plains

desc:
This high bank borders the road, down from here the road runs southwest to
northeast. You can see where the road has fallen into disrepair due to recent
heavy rains. It is rutted and full of many potholes. A tall stand of trees
borders the road on the north side.
name: On Top of a Sloping Bank

desc:
These plains are wide open and are fairly level. The grass here is about calf
length and blows gently with the breeze. Walking here is fairly easy. In the sky
you can see various birds flying high overhead, you can also hear them calling
to each other. Closer to the north you see a tall stand of trees, you realise
this is the forest that borders Old East Road. To the west is a high bank that
borders the road to the south. All other directions are wide open plains as far
as the eye can see.
name: Plains

desc:
Here the road turns southward. You see that wagon wheels have dug deep ruts into
the road and that they are filled with water from recent heavy rains. Bree
cannot be far away to the east.
name: Old East Road

desc:
These plains are wide open and are fairly level. The grass here is about half
length and blow gently with the breeze. Walking here is fairly easy. In the sky
you can see various birds flying high over head, you can also hear them calling
to each other. Far to the north you see a dark line that could be the trees
bordering the road. You are wandering around staring up at the sky at the birds
and you don't notice that you are not walking in a set direction. You just seem
to be taking a relaxing walk through the plains.
name: Plains

desc:
These plains are wide open and are fairly level. The grass here is about half
length and blow gently with the breeze. Walking here is fairly easy. In the sky
you can see various birds flying high over head, you can also hear them calling
to each other. Far to the north you see a dark line that could be the trees
bordering the road. You are wandering around staring up at the sky at the birds
and you don't notice that you are not walking in a set direction. You just seem
to be taking a relaxing walk through the plains.
name: Plains

desc:
These plains are wide open and are fairly level. The grass here is about calf
length and blow gently with the breeze. Walking here is fairly easy. In the sky
you can see various birds flying high over head, you can also hear them calling
to each other. Far to the north you see a dark line that could be the trees
bordering the road. You are wandering around staring up at the sky at the birds
and you don't notice that you are not walking in a set direction. You just seem
to be taking a relaxing walk through the plains.
name: Plains

desc:
These plains are wide open and are fairly level. The grass here is about half
length and blows gently with the breeze. Walking here is fairly easy. In the sky
you can see various birds flying high over head, you can also hear them calling
to each other. Far to the north you see a dark line that could be the trees
bordering the road. East there is a long wall, that used to mark some sort of
boundary. Travelling along this wall for a bit you find a hole big enough to
walk through.
name: Plains

desc:
You're walking through the thick wall that used to mark the boundaries to an old
and almost forgotten about kingdom from long ago. You realise there must be a
sad tale about why only the wall remains, but you think it's best to never ask
someone to tell you the story.
name: Hole in the Wall

desc:
On the far side of this deep dike you see a huge wall that used to mark the
boundaries of a kingdom. This kingdom is now in ruins and is buried beneath the
hills of the downs. The line you saw previously, was that of a line of bushes
that has grown along the edge of this dike. Travelling along side this wall you
find a exit big enough to walk through to the other side.
name: A Deep Dike

desc:
You have entered into a small hobbit hole without even knocking on the door! It
seems that the hobbits that live here will eat soon, there is lots of good food
on the table, and the kitchen looks like a mess. The man of the house seems to
be out at the moment, but since the food seems almost to be finished he'll no
doubt be home soon.
name: A Hobbit Hole

desc:
The shop is small, but the range of wares offered astonishes you. From pots to
spoons to lanterns, every conceivable item an average hobbit household may need
seems to be stocked. Perhaps the relative isolation of Dwaling accounts for this
self-sufficiency. A sign has been placed on the counter at the front of the
shop.
name: General Store

desc:
The village square appears peaceful in comparison with the bustling fish market,
though you are surrounded by shops selling various necessities of life - notably
food and cooking condiments. A big sign under a wide arch on your east proclaims
the entrance to the only and therefore popular inn for miles around.
name: Village Square of Dwaling

desc:
You stand in the small courtyard of the Waterside Inn, reputed for miles around
as the favourite meeting spot of the queer fisherhobbits and other equally queer
hobbits who fool with boats on the River. On your east, some low steps lead up
to a wide open doorway from which the mingled smell of beer and pipeweed
emerges. The stables lie north.
name: Courtyard of the Waterside Inn

desc:
You have entered the smoky common room of the Inn. Hobbits sit in groups at the
large round tables drinking, smoking and telling tales. Now and then a voice
rises above the din, calling out for one more round of beer. At the north end of
the room, a small but well-stocked bar is recessed against the wall, beside a
dim corridor which leads into the back wing of the Inn. A sign is on the bar
counter.
name: The Waterside Inn

desc:
You are in the back wing of the Inn, where the guest rooms are located. The Inn
seems to have been built just for hobbits, for two rows of round doors stretch
off as far as you can see. A small counter has been set partway across the
corridor such as to partially block it, and a sign is displayed prominently on
the counter.
name: The Inn's Rooms

desc:
You see a row of hay-lined stalls, some with ponies in them. The stalls look
clean, tidy and comfortable, the ponies content and well-looked after. This will
be a good place to leave your mounts and pets. You see a painted sign nailed to
one of the stalls. A cheerful stablehand greets you as you enter.
name: The Inn's Stables

desc:
A delicious smell of baking greets you as you enter. Your attention is
immediately drawn to the glass display case from within which a tantalizing
array of fresh pastries, cakes and breads tempt you. A big sign rests on top of
the display case.
name: Bakery

desc:
You seem to have entered some sort of storage shed. Empty barrels stand piled in
one corner, fishing nets and rods lie in another, and other boxes of odd shapes
and sizes line the entire length of one wall. Surprisingly, there is no
sensation of floating. The damp floorboards are the only indication that you are
not on firm ground.
name: Fish Farm

desc:
The inside of this room surprises you. The floor - or what there is of it -
consists of long planks laid criss-cross over the water surface at wide
intervals, such that square islands of water are formed between them.
Fine-meshed nets extend down from under the planks to form underwater
barricades. You see various types of fish in the clear water.
name: Fish Farm

desc:
You stand in the Fish Market of Dwaling, famed in all the Shire for its constant
supply of fresh fish. Barrels and crates arranged in distinct groups mark the
territories of the fish sellers - or fishtraders as the Dwaling folk call them.
On your north, rows of neat hobbit houses line the path to the village square.
name: Fish Market of Dwaling

desc:
You are on a wooden landing which extends a little way onto the River. A few
small boats are moored on one side of it. Some fisherhobbits are gathered beside
these boats, loudly discussing their latest catches. On your north, a wooden
structure very much like a small shed has been built on the River itself.
name: A Landing

desc:
You are on the River Brandywine which forms the east border of the Shire. To
your west, a wooden landing which straddles a part of the water links the River
with the bustling village you can see. Except for the occasional splashes and
waves caused by hobbit activity on the numerous boats moored to the landing, the
water is extremely calm. Indeed the stillness of the water leads you to believe
you are in a sheltered bay.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You have entered the living quarters of the shopkeeper. The furnishing is simple
and comfortable, but not what you would expect in a hobbit home. An old fishing
net is draped over some mathoms displayed on the mantelpiece, and the hat stand
in the corner looks suspiciously like old fishing rods bound together. A round
window in the northern wall looks out on the calm water of the River Brandywine.
name: Living Quarters

desc:
Shelves upon shelves of fishing rods, bits, tackles and other fishing equipment
take up most of the space here. A low counter stands at the front of the store,
with a sign on it. In the far wall at the back, a round door catches your
attention. You wonder what it opens to.
name: Fishing Supplies Store

desc:
You're shocked to find a room inside of a well, and you wonder why it was built.
As you look around at the meagre valuables stored here, you begin to understand
why. The ceiling of this room is held up with strong beams of wood, so the room
is quite secure from cave-in. The floor is of shiny flagstone, that hasn't had
much use. You see some arm shackles hanging by some rusty, heavy chains.
name: A Damp and Musty Secret Room

desc:
YUCK!!!!!! Your foot sinks down into the slime and mud of the neglected pig pen.
You begin to sink lower, and soon you're almost knee deep in this mess. The pigs
are not happy with your invasion of their happy home, and begin to move towards
you.
name: A Slimy, Messy Pig Pen

desc:
You gasp for air as the dust from this broken down old barn fills your lungs.
From the light rays filtering in from the cracks of the rotten boards barely
holding this barn together, you are able to watch huge dust particles flow
around the room. Through the haze, you see that the floor seems to be littered
with objects. Also, in the far northwestern corner of the barn, you can barely
make out the ladder obscured by this polluted air.
name: Inside a Run-down Rejected Barn

desc:
The floor of this loft creaks and groans under your weight. Some of the floor
boards seem barely able to support your steps. In the roof you see one small
hole that leaks when it rains. There is a musty smell of some wet hay, but for
the most part the hay is dry. Some of the bundles of hay are intact, and the
outer straws are yellowed with age. You notice in one corner of the rafters
above you, there seems to be a large nest. You can hear the faint sound of barn
mice scurrying within the hay.
name: The Hayloft

desc:
You walk on a narrow trail which seems to have lain undiscovered for ages. Rows
of tall trees line both sides of the trail, leaving just enough room for two to
walk abreast. Overhead the tree branches form a canopy which sways in the wind,
causing the sunlight filtering through to constantly shift and move shadows
around. To your east, the trail leads into and under the hills.
name: A Hidden Trail

desc:
You have come to the southern end of the Hills of Scary. Here, the terrain seems
rockier, and there is a noticeable thinning of the grass which covers the
ground. In some places there are only occasional spots of grass between larger
patches of bare soil. Looking down the steep western slope, you see what appears
to be a quarry, but there is no way down. On your east and south are sheer drops
down to a flat stretch of grass.
name: The Southern Hill

desc:
You are standing on the Old East Road, just east of the Brandywine bridge. A
dusty road leads to a gate in a tall hedgerow to the south - the High Hay. It
was grown to protect Buckland from the old forest many years ago, and is thick
and nearly impenetrable.
name: East of the Bridge

desc:
You are on the only path to Dwaling, which stretches straight east-west for some
distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland
and undulating hills create a scenic contrast.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You are on the only path to Dwaling, which stretches straight east-west for some
distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland
and undulating hills create a scenic contrast.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You are on the only path to Dwaling, which stretches straight east-west for some
distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland
and undulating hills create a scenic contrast.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You see an endless expanse of grass extending northwards from the path to the
south, broken only by a few scattered trees. Near the path, the grass reaches
only to your ankles, but as you trudge on, more and more of your body gets
submerged in the green sea. You have no choice but to retreat to the civilized
path.
name: Grassland

desc:
You see an endless expanse of grass extending northwards from the path to the
south, broken only by a few scattered trees. Near the path, the grass reaches
only to your ankles, but as you trudge on, more and more of your body gets
submerged in the green sea. You have no choice but to retreat to the civilised
path.
name: Grassland

desc:
You see an endless expanse of grass extending northwards from the path to the
south, broken only by a few scattered trees. Near the path, the grass reaches
only to your ankles, but as you trudge on, more and more of your body gets
submerged in the green sea. You have no choice but to retreat to the civilised
path.
name: Grassland

desc:
You are on the only path to Dwaling, which makes a turn to the north and west
here around the hills. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of
wheels and hooves suggest it is well-used. A little further north, you can see
that the path makes another turn to the east, but the high hill on this side
prevents you from seeing beyond that.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You have reached the southern end of the narrow ridge, flanked by sheer drops on
all three sides. The clear water of the River Brandywine is now directly below
you on the east. On your west and south, a grassy field meets the foot of the
hills.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
Thick grass and pretty wild flowers make these meadows an attractive pasture.
Above you to the north is one of the steep bluffs that mark the edges of the
Hills of Scary. Eastward is a green hill, and beyond it must be the Brandywine.
name: Bridgefields

desc:
This place is well within the security of the light forest. There are many paths
to follow from here, though some less travelled than others. The main path
heading to the east looks like the easiest to traverse.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
The long path to Dwaling makes a final turn to the east, before ending at two
low signposts which are apparently meant to mark some sort of entrance to the
village. The strong smell of fish is unmistakable even from here, so it is not
hard to guess the main trade of Dwaling. A small, well-used path leads north
from here.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
From the outside this may look like a place where someone lives, but once inside
it is pretty clear this wooden structure is nothing but a temporary shelter for
workmen and the like. Tools, most of them related to the breeding and keeping of
horses lie neatly ordered on a large nearby bench. To the east a large doorway
leads out towards a corral. South of here another exit opens up towards the
village of Dwaling.
name: Large Barn

desc:
You are walking on seemingly endless fields that lead far north. To the south
you can see a light forest, and still further the Old East road winds its way
west to the Shire, and east to Bree.
name: Fields

desc:
You are walking on seemingly endless fields that lead far north. To the south
you can see a light forest, and still further the Old East road winds its way
west to the Shire, and east to Bree.
name: Fields

desc:
You are walking on seemingly endless fields that lead far north. To the south
you can see a light forest, and still further the Old East road winds its way
west to the Shire, and east to Bree.
name: Fields

desc:
This small trail climbs up a gentle hill just north-west of the small hobbit
village of Dwaling. Low shrubs and bushes grow along the path, and the path
itself is littered with large helpings of fresh horse manure. The main entrance
to the village lies south of here, while a large building stands to the north.
name: Path through a Field

desc:
The room appears to be furnished quite luxuriously, hinting at the owner's
kinship to hobbits. Large shelves completely cover one wall of the room, their
space filled up with numerous books, the actual amount difficult to grasp. A
round fireplace has been built in the middle of the room, and a rocking chair
stands close to it, little pillows cushioning the seat. Torches flicker in their
hangers near the doorways to the north and west.
name: A Dimly Lit Livingroom

desc:
A small, round window is the only source of light in this tiny hobbit hovel. The
hovel has a small kitchenette, bed, and a small cupboard for household
essentials. Everything is miniscule and simple, yet in perfect condition and
immaculately clean.
name: Pintsize's Modest Hobbit Home

desc:
A wall to wall carpet covers the floor of this sleeping chamber. A wide oaken
bed has been set against one wall, covered with hand-sewn blankets and dozens of
tiny pillows. The colours of both the blankets and pillows vary with all shades
of the rainbow, showing the master's extravagant taste. A tiny bedside table
sits right beside the bed, a half-used candlestick resting on it. Near the
opposite wall stands a sturdy cupboard, obviously for keeping the personal
belongings of its habitant.
name: Sleeping Chamber

desc:
After squeezing in from the low doorway to the east, one finds a well-organized
pantry, even if it is a tad over-stuffed. Shelves line all the walls apart from
the doorway, their wooden boards just barely sagging from the amount of food and
goods that are set on them. Glass jugs contain sweets as well as salads and
preserved vegetables. Wooden boxes contain fresh foods and smoked meat. On one
shelf stands a row of tiny sacks of herbs, strange aromas wafting off of them.
Aged oaken barrels have been stacked on top of each other, filling up the
crammed pantry even more.
name: A Small Pantry

desc:
A thatched roof, supported by wooden pillars, hangs above a third of this
sizeable and well-kept corral. A large barn can be seen to the west, where a big
doorway leads into a shady, cool space. The rest of the corral is surrounded by
a high fence that serves to keep predators out, and anyone inside it... inside.
Stacks of hay lie piled up underneath the roofing, providing ample food for any
animal kept in here.
name: Partly-covered Corral

desc:
Here, the river passes by the small village of Witfurrows, built on its shore.
You can see that its houses are very small and could hardly be used by any Man.
A ford forces you to leave the river if you want to keep sailing.
name: The Water

desc:
Parts of the walls of this house are living trees which fit perfectly into gaps
between the wooden planks. This house is just one large square room. Every
available spot seems to be covered with shelves, pots, jars, glasses, and
baskets containing potions, herbs, plants, and powders. It looks as if someone
has worked here for years, but rarely if ever tidies the place.
name: Old Druid's House

desc:
Stout but simple wooden bookshelves line the walls of this small chamber.
Seemingly disorganised stacks of old, dusty books fill the shelves and large
parts of the floor. An old, heavy armchair stands beside the only window in this
room. The small fireplace is filled with the embers of a dying fire.
name: Library

desc:
A tunnel of loose dirt leads down into a pool of foul black water a foot deep.
The pool fills the entire hole, getting deeper further away from the entrance.
The air smells sour and rotten, and is so thick with dankness that it collects
on any dry thing that passes through. At the far south end, an ooze of slime
clogs the wall at the water level.
name: Flooded Hole

desc:
Thick green slime covers the bottom of this small tunnel, the walls oozing a
foul coating ever down. In the north, the tunnel descends into some foul water
where a clog of slime blocks the way. In the south, the tunnel continues into
the darkness. About, the air is thick with moisture and a dank, sour smell.
name: Slimy Tunnel

desc:
Sludge walls turn to muck as they give way to the dank, wet slime that covers
the floor. The floor is thick and gooey, slurping anything that touches it.
Green drips of foetid water fall from above, and the air is wet and stale. The
slime below is marked by animal footprints.
name: Slimy Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel slants down slightly as it hits a dead end, creating a small cavern.
A short ceiling is crowded, round and clammy, oozing with muck that slides down
the walls. The thickening slime creeps to a deep and soupy bottom. Drops of
rank, green goo drip down, splattering slime with irregular plops.
name: Pool of Slime

desc:
Partway up a huge tree, approximately six yard from the ground, the branches and
leaves are so dense that it is almost impossible to discern anything on the
ground below. Everything is so thick and dense that climbing is no easy task,
and is indeed very taxing, though not impossible.
name: Partway up a Huge Tree

desc:
The top of the huge tree does provide somewhat of a vantage point to the low,
rolling plains in this area, but not to the immediate surroundings. The branches
and leaves below are too thick to discern anything on the ground directly below.
name: Top of a Huge Tree

desc:
A grand chamber stands unsteady, light seeping in through cracks in the walls
and great holes above. The building had a second story at one point, but it has
fallen onto this one. Large beams lay smashed on the left and fore, crushing the
walls and exposing the room to the elements. Dust covers the ground, and the
boards creak loudly. All about, vines reach in, thick and greedy.
name: Rickety Residence

desc:
As opposed to the rest of these ruins, this room is intact. The ceiling is
white, spotted with black bits of mould, and in the corners, thick spider webs
glom together. The outer wall's window is broken glass, smashed by ropy, thorny
vines. On the walls are hung pots, pans, knives, spoons, and shelves of spices.
name: Ruined Kitchen

desc:
This small room sits off the kitchen, dark and cramped. Two beds bunk against
the far wall, straw poking through thin, yellow sheets. The walls are painted
ochre, and drip with water stains. An ancient, mouldy grey rug covers the wooden
floor, and fills the room with a sour odour.
name: Servants' Quarters

desc:
Stairs lead down into a large food cellar, the creaking wood still sturdy enough
to hold weight. The room is man-high, lined with stones, and has a dirt floor.
Thick wooden beams hold up the first floor, and large cracks show in the wood.
The room smells thickly of mould and stale flour.
name: Mouldy Cellar

desc:
The fast flowing stream moves wildly about. The current becomes very strong in
areas and pulls with great force on everything within the water. Sand and
stones, caught in the current, rush by and crack loudly as they constantly erode
the walls into a smooth texture.
name: Subterranean Torrent

desc:
The strong current grabs hold of anything caught in it. Without effort, rocks
are carried away by the rushing water. Proceeding against the fast flowing
stream seems hard, since the smooth, wet walls offer nearly no handholds at all.
name: Subterranean Torrent

desc:
The underground stream rushes through a narrow, low tunnel. The current is very
strong, and carries away anything that gets caught within it. The walls are
covered with some light-shimmering green moss, making for a smooth, slippery
surface. The low ceiling is smooth as well, offering no handhold for relief from
the turbulent current.
name: Subterranean Torrent

desc:
The narrow tunnel has caved in to the west. Boulders and rocks have fallen to
block the passage from going any further. The current is still strong as the
water flows through vacancies in between these rocks. There seems to be no way
to remove the fallen stones and follow the path of the water downstream.
name: Underground River

desc:
The bank of the river is grey and sandy. A few sharp rocks scattered throughout
the bank can be seen sticking from the sand. The cavern measures maybe fifteen
feet in length, but is just about four feet deep. A jagged hole can be spotted
in the cavern's roof. However, no handholds can be spotted in the rocky walls
that would lead upward.
name: Small Beach

desc:
This circular chamber looks like a place where its owner comes to rest when
needing some distance from the affairs of the outer world. The room has a cosy
bed, a table and a couple of chairs. Being a hobbit hole, the most dominant
pieces of furniture are two magnificent cupboards, filled with all kind of food.
There are two paintings on the wall: the first one represents a cunning looking
hobbit, and another, bigger one, illustrates a well-known hobbit tale from the
old ages. Several books can be found on shelves located around the room.
name: Rael's Alcove

desc:
Still lost within the realm of the Old Forest you begin to become paranoid of
the ill will from the trees. You want to break the silence, but you know
shouting will not help, the air seems to stifle your voice. You constantly have
the impression that something is following you, or all the trees seem to be
watching you. This steadily grows on your nerves, and you grow weary, almost as
if you become content with the fate of being lost forever in this evil wood...
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:

name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You notice that there seems to be more space between the trees and it is not so
threatening. Seems as if the ill will of the trees has lessened considerably at
this point. The air is easier to breath, and off in the distance you hear the
sound of birds calling each other. You breath a sigh of relief when you notice
that the road is only a mile away and there seems to be a wide path with grass
borders leading to the road. Seems as if you are home free now!
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:

name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You've reached a small clearing in the wood. Even though you can see the sky for
the first time in a long time, you still cannot see how far you must travel to
reach the road again. All around this clearing the trees grow thick, and it is
dark within their depths. The ground here is mushy and bog like and your feet
sink below the surface into the wet grass. This is by no means a place to rest
very long, for you feel like the trees are once again ready to occupy the very
spot you are standing on.
name: A Small Clearing in the Wood

desc:
You are wandering along a path that meanders into a light woodland. As you peer
into the increasing darkness in the south, you begin to shiver uncontrollably.
The trees seem to age and appear unfriendly in that direction. They seem also to
be warning you to turn back in the direction from which you have arrived.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Road

desc:
The trees reluctantly part before you to reveal a hidden path. You have the
feeling that the forest will fight your movement every step of the way. As you
travel a couple of paces into the forest you feel a heavy wind behind you, a
wind that is caused by great movement. You turn around and to your surprise the
trees have trapped you inside. You've only the path to follow before you. You
hear snickering, and it seems to come from the trees.
name: A Path Leading into the Forest

desc:
You sense that you are following a path that is chosen for you. As if the old
forest decides which direction you are to take. If you try to stray from the
chosen path, you sense the anger of the trees as they quickly move in to block
your way. You constantly feel like you are being watched.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest
